title: Reverse Words in a String
description: Given a string s, reverse the order of the words. A word is defined as a sequence of non-space characters. The words in s will be separated by at least one space and may have leading or trailing spaces. The final output should not contain any extra spaces. Return the reversed string of words.

examples.explanation: The input string has leading and trailing spaces. After removing them, we get 'Hello World!'. Reversing the order of words gives 'World! Hello'.

examples.input: "The quick brown fox jumps over the lazy dog"
examples.output: "dog lazy the over jumps fox brown quick The"
examples.explanation: The input string contains multiple words. Reversing the order yields 'dog lazy the over jumps fox brown quick The'.
Extra spaces between words are ignored. Thus, 'a good example' becomes 'example good a' after reversing.

constraints: The input string s has a length of at most 10^4 characters.
Words are separated by one or more spaces.
hints: Consider using two pointers to help with reversing the words.
First, split the string into words before processing.
Be mindful of leading and trailing spaces in the final result.
Try to avoid using built-in functions that directly reverse strings.
